expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (101 lines) and architecture.md (86 lines) both contain substantive content

expectations.text: A1: Correct architecture recommendation (simple style with evolution)
expectations.evidence: Selected: Modular Monolith (Phase 1) -> Service-Based (Phase 2) -> Selective Event-Driven (Phase 3). Correctly identifies the structural conflict between simplicity and scalability/elasticity and resolves via phased evolution.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Table in Step 2 ranks all 8: Service-Based, Modular Monolith, Microkernel, Layered, Event-Driven, Space-Based, Microservices, Service-Oriented

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 has 4 structural FFs: No circular module dependencies, Module boundary enforcement (no internal imports), Single deployment artifact, Database schema per module. Phase 2 adds 4 service-based structural FFs. Phase 3 adds 5 event-driven structural FFs. All are structural invariants, not characteristic metrics.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 FFs are modular-monolith-specific (module boundaries, single artifact). Phase 2 FFs are service-based-specific (service count limits, chatter limits, DB sharing discipline). Phase 3 FFs are event-driven-specific (no sync coupling, schema registry, consumer idempotency). Each set is distinct and appropriate to its style.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach column
expectations.evidence: Tool/Approach column present for all three phases: dependency-cruiser/ArchUnit, package visibility/lint rules, build script check, SQL analysis, directory/package structure check, runtime tracing, schema registry check, DLQ configuration check

expectations.text: A6: Fitness functions scoped to current phase, future FFs mentioned but not mandatory
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 FFs clearly labeled as current ('Phase 1: Modular Monolith (Current)'). Phase 2 FFs explicitly labeled 'Future -- activates when Phase 2 begins'. Phase 3 FFs labeled 'Future -- activates when Phase 3 begins, for hot-path components only'. Perfect phase scoping.
